title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for bytes, unicode, newlines, front matter, metadata, and schemas and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned decoded-document and metadata contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.
Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Time Language Identity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 1493 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Time Language Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable ids without locale ambiguity. For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Time Language Identity, keep Time Language Identity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Explicit Decoding has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Explicit Decoding, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Explicit Decoding precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Explicit Decoding, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 9781 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.

For Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Explicit Decoding, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Markdown files are text, so encoding cannot affect a build.

options.text: Declare Static-site publishing and Python reader outcome, content authority, revision, source identity, parser scope, trust policy, route base, accessibility need, and byte, depth, work, and output budgets for the quiz Explicit Decoding case

Predict Static-site publishing and Python admission, decoding, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the quiz Explicit Decoding case

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Time Language Identity
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Time Language Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 4924 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Time Language Identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Time Language Identity, keep Time Language Identity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent dates, time zones, draft state, language tags, translations, authors, and stable IDs without locale ambiguity.
